chunk_text: Annual Leave Policy. Purpose: To provide clear rules for requesting, approving, and carrying forward annual leave. Eligibility: Full-time employees become eligible for annual leave after successfully completing a 3-month probation period.
retrieval_questions: How many annual leave days do I have each year?

chunk_text: Rule 1: Eligible employees receive 12 days of paid annual leave per calendar year. Rule 2: Leave requests should be submitted through the HR system at least 3 working days in advance.
retrieval_questions: How many annual leave days do I have each year?

chunk_text: Rule 1: All annual leave requests require direct supervisor approval before the leave date. Rule 2: Unused annual leave may be carried forward for up to 3 months into the next calendar year with management approval.
retrieval_questions: Can I carry forward unused annual leave?

chunk_text: Urgent personal circumstances may allow shorter notice, subject to supervisor approval.
retrieval_questions: How early do I need to apply for annual leave?

chunk_text: Sick Leave Policy. Purpose: To define notification and documentation requirements when employees are absent due to illness. Eligibility: All employees may apply for sick leave in accordance with company rules and local employment regulations.
retrieval_questions: Do I need a medical certificate for one day of sick leave?

chunk_text: Rule 1: Employees should notify their direct supervisor before the start of the working day whenever possible. Rule 2: Sick leave of 1 to 2 consecutive days may be self-reported through the HR system.
retrieval_questions: Do I need a medical certificate for one day of sick leave?

chunk_text: Rule 1: A valid medical certificate is required for sick leave exceeding 2 consecutive days. Rule 2: Medical certificates should be submitted within 2 working days after the employee returns to work.
retrieval_questions: When should I inform my manager if I am sick?

chunk_text: HR may request additional review if sick leave is repeatedly used in a suspicious or inconsistent pattern.
retrieval_questions: What happens if I take sick leave for more than 2 days?

chunk_text: Attendance and Late Arrival Policy. Purpose: To set expectations for punctual attendance and consistent availability during working hours. Eligibility: This policy applies to all office-based employees unless otherwise specified by their department.
retrieval_questions: What time am I expected to start work?

chunk_text: Rule 1: Standard working hours are 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, Monday to Friday, with a 1-hour lunch break. Rule 2: Arriving more than 10 minutes late without a valid reason is recorded as late attendance.
retrieval_questions: What time am I expected to start work?

chunk_text: Rule 1: More than 3 instances of lateness in one month may trigger a reminder from the supervisor. Rule 2: Repeated lateness or unapproved early departure may be considered during performance review.
retrieval_questions: What counts as being late?

chunk_text: Approved flexible arrangements or documented emergencies may be exempt from standard lateness treatment.
retrieval_questions: Will repeated lateness affect my performance review?

chunk_text: Overtime Compensation Policy. Purpose: To regulate overtime work and clarify how approved overtime is recorded and compensated. Eligibility: This policy applies to employees whose roles and contracts allow overtime compensation.
retrieval_questions: Do I need approval before working overtime?

chunk_text: Rule 1: Overtime should only be worked when business needs require it. Rule 2: All overtime must be approved in advance by the employee's direct supervisor.
retrieval_questions: Do I need approval before working overtime?

chunk_text: Rule 1: Approved overtime may be compensated through time off in lieu, subject to team scheduling needs. Rule 2: Overtime records must be submitted through the attendance system within 5 working days.
retrieval_questions: How is approved overtime compensated?

chunk_text: Unapproved overtime is generally not eligible for compensation unless management confirms exceptional circumstances.
retrieval_questions: What if I forget to record my overtime hours?

chunk_text: Employee Reimbursement Policy. Purpose: To define how employees submit and receive reimbursement for approved business expenses. Eligibility: All employees may claim approved business-related expenses incurred for company purposes.
retrieval_questions: What expenses can I reimburse?

chunk_text: Rule 1: Only approved business expenses such as transport, client meeting costs, or office supplies may be reimbursed. Rule 2: Claims must be submitted through the finance system within 30 calendar days of the expense date.
retrieval_questions: What expenses can I reimburse?

chunk_text: Rule 1: Valid receipts or invoices must be uploaded for each expense item. Rule 2: Claims require supervisor approval before finance review and payment.
retrieval_questions: How long do I have to submit a reimbursement claim?

chunk_text: Claims without supporting documents may be rejected unless exceptional approval is granted.
retrieval_questions: Do I need to upload a receipt for reimbursement?

chunk_text: Business Travel Claim Policy. Purpose: To provide standards for pre-approval, spending limits, and reimbursement of business travel expenses. Eligibility: Employees traveling for approved business purposes may claim eligible travel expenses.
retrieval_questions: Do I need approval before booking a business trip?

chunk_text: Rule 1: Manager approval is required before any business trip is booked. Rule 2: Eligible expenses include economy-class transport, standard hotel accommodation, and reasonable meal costs within company limits.
retrieval_questions: Do I need approval before booking a business trip?

chunk_text: Rule 1: Employees must retain receipts, boarding passes, and invoices where applicable. Rule 2: Travel claims should be submitted within 10 working days after the trip is completed.
retrieval_questions: Can I claim hotel and meal expenses during business travel?

chunk_text: Expenses above standard limits require written justification and additional department approval.
retrieval_questions: When do I need to submit my travel claim?

policy_title: Office Supplies and Asset Request Policy
chunk_text: Office Supplies and Asset Request Policy. Purpose: To standardize requests for office supplies and company-owned devices or equipment. Eligibility: All employees may request supplies or assets needed for approved business use.
retrieval_questions: How do I request a new office laptop?

policy_title: Office Supplies and Asset Request Policy
chunk_text: Rule 1: Requests must be submitted through the administration system with a business reason. Rule 2: Standard office supplies may be approved by department coordinators.
retrieval_questions: How do I request a new office laptop?

policy_title: Office Supplies and Asset Request Policy
chunk_text: Rule 1: Higher-value assets such as laptops or mobile devices require supervisor and administration approval. Rule 2: All assigned assets remain company property and must be recorded in the asset register.
retrieval_questions: Who approves office equipment requests?

policy_title: Office Supplies and Asset Request Policy
chunk_text: Employees must return assigned assets in good condition when transferring roles, upgrading devices, or resigning.
retrieval_questions: Do I need to return company assets when I resign?

policy_title: Employee Account and Email Access Policy
chunk_text: Employee Account and Email Access Policy. Purpose: To control issuance, use, and deactivation of company accounts and system permissions. Eligibility: All employees receive access based on job responsibilities and approved business needs.
retrieval_questions: When will I receive my company email account?

policy_title: Employee Account and Email Access Policy
chunk_text: Rule 1: Company email and system accounts are created after onboarding is completed. Rule 2: Access permissions are granted according to role and business necessity.
retrieval_questions: When will I receive my company email account?

policy_title: Employee Account and Email Access Policy
chunk_text: Rule 1: Employees must keep login credentials confidential and must not share accounts. Rule 2: Additional access requests must go through the IT request process with manager approval.
retrieval_questions: Can I share my login with a teammate?

policy_title: Employee Account and Email Access Policy
chunk_text: IT will update or deactivate access when an employee changes role, transfers, or resigns.
retrieval_questions: How do I apply for additional system access?

chunk_text: Software Installation and Approval Policy. Purpose: To prevent security and licensing risks caused by unauthorized software on company devices. Eligibility: This policy applies to all employees who use company-owned computers or managed devices.
retrieval_questions: Can I install software on my company laptop by myself?

chunk_text: Rule 1: Employees may not independently install software, apps, or browser extensions on company devices. Rule 2: Software requests must be submitted through the IT request system with a business justification.
retrieval_questions: Can I install software on my company laptop by myself?

chunk_text: Rule 1: The IT department reviews requests for licensing, compatibility, cybersecurity risk, and compliance. Rule 2: Only approved and legally licensed software may be installed on company devices.
retrieval_questions: How do I request a new software tool?

chunk_text: Unauthorized installations may be removed and may trigger additional review if security risks are identified.
retrieval_questions: Why does software installation need IT approval?

policy_title: Password Reset and Device Security Policy
chunk_text: Password Reset and Device Security Policy. Purpose: To define password requirements and the actions employees must take if devices are lost or compromised. Eligibility: All employees must follow password and device security controls when using company systems.
retrieval_questions: What should I do if I forget my password?

policy_title: Password Reset and Device Security Policy
chunk_text: Rule 1: Passwords must meet company security standards, including minimum length and complexity requirements. Rule 2: Passwords must not be shared or reused across unauthorized systems.
retrieval_questions: What should I do if I forget my password?

policy_title: Password Reset and Device Security Policy
chunk_text: Rule 1: Multi-factor authentication must be enabled for supported company platforms. Rule 2: Lost, stolen, or compromised devices must be reported to IT immediately.
retrieval_questions: Do I need to use multi-factor authentication?

policy_title: Password Reset and Device Security Policy
chunk_text: IT may remotely lock or wipe a device and restrict access while assessing potential security risks.
retrieval_questions: What happens if my company laptop is lost?
